steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers argue that "invasive" and "native" are labels for temporary circumstances — climate change shifts species ranges and creates new habitats. The correct answer must apply this logic to Canada's classification of the round goby as invasive.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "the country was previously home to some round gobies but they were outcompeted by invading species.": References a past state not mentioned in the passage — the researchers' argument is about the future, not a prior classification. ✗
  • "the country's designation of the round goby as invasive may be appropriate now but not in the future.": Canada's "invasive" label may be appropriate now but may become obsolete as climate change reshapes habitats — directly applying the researchers' logic. ✓
  • "it's useful at present for the country to distinguish between invasive and native species in some instances but not in the case of the round goby.": Suggests the invasive/native distinction itself is invalid, but the passage only says the labels are temporary, not meaningless. ✗
  • "even if Earth's climate doesn't change in the way scientists predict, the round goby will likely establish itself in the country.": Contradicts the climate-change logic by implying the label is permanent or fixed. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
